project: Na Pua Makani Wind Farm (8 turbines ~24-27.6 MW)
developer: AES / Na Pua Makani Power Partners
groups: Keep the North Shore Country; Kahuku Community Association; Kahuku residents
counsel: Lance D. Collins; Bianca K. Isaki (community). Carlsmith Ball (Manaut/Thoene) and Yamamoto Caliboso (applicant)
forum: Street protest; BLNR contested case; Circuit Court; ICA; Hawaii Supreme Court
mechanism: Road blockade + ~165-200 arrests (2019); contested-case challenge to habitat conservation plan / incidental-take license; appeals of DPP permits, waiver, ZBA rulings
outcome: Community lost every merits ruling: SC affirmed HCP (SCAP-19-0000449, 2022-02-25); ICA affirmed permit dismissal (CAAP-21-0000396 SDO, 2025-03-27); SC rejected cert 2025 (SCWC-21-0000396, dissent 2025-07-23). Turbines built and operating since 2020
notes: Largest street mobilization against any HI renewable project. Community lost in court but won at the ordinance level (see Ord 25-2 row). Protesters said they would have supported solar instead.

developer: (regulatory, all Oahu wind incl. Kahuku Wind, Na Pua Makani, Kawailoa)
groups: Kahuku Community Association (Sunny Unga); North Shore residents; attorney Ryan D. Hurley (self)
mechanism: Multi-year ordinance-driving petition + testimony campaign: Res 19-305 (5 mi) -> Bills 28/29/30 (2021) -> Bills 10/55 (2022) -> Bill 64 (2023) -> Ord 25-2
outcome: Enacted 2025-01-03: setback = greater of 10x tip height or 1.25 mi from country/residential/apartment/resort lots; DPP waivers banned; repowering capped. 18 of 20 Kahuku turbines nonconforming; Oahu wind fleet expected retired 2031-2040
notes: Clearest case of community resistance WINNING via the legislative/ordinance forum. Enacted rule far exceeds the original grievance geometry; makes most of Oahu infeasible for wind. State bills HB 2188 (2020), SB 957 (2021) died.

groups: NONE (on-site cultural/archaeological sites, not an organized group)
mechanism: On-site cultural-resource discovery; downsizing attempts failed
notes: The only Oahu solar project ever stopped by anything permit/land-like -- but by on-site cultural resources, NOT organized community opposition. Distinguished from the Maui pattern.

project: AES West Oahu Solar (12.5 MW) SP21-411
groups: ~10-16 individual residents; Dr. Kioni Dudley (individual testimony)
forum: LUC special-use permit / Honolulu Planning Commission
mechanism: ~10-16 near-identical opposition emails (small coordinated email effort, all individuals -- no organization); individual oral/written testimony
notes: Individual (not organized) opposition; included to mark the boundary between individual testimony and organized resistance. No group, no intervention, no litigation.

project: Oahu utility-scale solar generally (Mahi, Hoohana, Mililani, Waiawa, Kawailoa, AES West Oahu, Kupono, Barbers Point)
forum: (all venues swept: LUC, PUC, county PC, courts)
mechanism: NULL -- no organized community group, no neighborhood-board opposition resolution, no contested-case intervention, no lawsuit found against any Oahu utility-scale solar project
outcome: No organized resistance located in the record
notes: STRONG NULL. Oahu solar dies of economics in HECO procurement, never of opposition. Mass mobilization on Oahu went to WIND (Kahuku), not solar. Absence of evidence in searched record; residual gaps = unlogged county records.

location: Ulupalakua / above Maui Meadows, South Maui
groups: Pono Power Coalition (700+ members); Maui Meadows Neighborhood Association; Maui Tomorrow Foundation; Hooponopono O Makena
counsel: Lance D. Collins + Bianca K. Isaki (PUC/appeal); Christina D. Lizzi + Linda Nye and Ryan Hurley (county intervenors)
forum: PUC contested case (docket 2018-0433); Hawaii Supreme Court (SCOT-21-0000041); Maui Planning Commission; 2nd Circuit Court
mechanism: PUC participant status; appeal; county SUP/Phase-II permit challenge after intervention denied
outcome: Developer won on merits (PUC D&O 37340 approved; SC affirmed 2022-03-02); BUT 2nd Circuit (Judge Kawano) VACATED county permits Jan 2022 and remanded for intervention. PPA jointly terminated 2023-11-28 citing legal-delay + pandemic cost/supply-chain
notes: CANONICAL CASE. Only solar land-use permit ever vacated by a HI court. Opposition did not win on merits -- the DELAY it injected collided with cost inflation and killed the project. Intervention right = a delay weapon.

counsel: Ryan D. Hurley + Bianca K. Isaki (WMPA); Schlack Ito (applicant)
outcome: Oct 2021 settlement: $1.375M (~$55k/yr x 25 yr) community-benefits package + covenant not to oppose future permits; WMPA withdrew; PUC approved. Project then withdrawn 2022-12-30 for pricing/supply-chain
notes: Opposition MONETIZED via an unprecedented community-benefits agreement, not a block. Economics finished the project after the settlement. Cancellation 2022-12-30 (corrected from earlier May-2022 dating).

project: Pulehu Solar (40 MW); Kamaole Solar (40 MW)
mechanism: NULL -- cancelled for supply-chain/inflation economics; no organized opposition documented
outcome: Both cancelled (Pulehu 2022-05-04; Kamaole among 4 Maui cancellations reported Nov 2023)
notes: Included to show that even on Maui, most solar deaths are economic, not opposition. Of 5 Maui PUC-approved solar projects 2018-2023, 4 died; only 1 (Paeahu) had organized opposition.

project: Hu Honua / Honua Ola Bioenergy (~21.5 MW biomass)
counsel: Lance D. Collins (2019 appeal); Chase H. Livingston (2023 appeal); Bronster Fujichaku Robbins / Bays Lung / Yamamoto Caliboso (applicant); Kobayashi Sugita & Goda (HELCO)
forum: PUC contested case (docket 2017-0122); Hawaii Supreme Court (two appeals)
mechanism: Intervention + two Supreme Court appeals on GHG / public-trust / clean-and-healthful-environment grounds
outcome: 1st appeal SCOT-17-0000630 (2019) vacated PPA approval; on remand PUC DENIED the PPA; 2nd appeal SCOT-22-0000418 (2023-03-13) AFFIRMED the denial. Project killed
notes: The single biggest NGO-opposition litigation WIN against a HI utility-scale renewable project. Biomass, not solar/wind, but squarely in scope. MW figure ~21.5 net / 30 gross -- exact figure UNVERIFIED here.

location: Ocean View / Hawaiian Ocean View Ranchos, Kau (D/E land)
project: SPI Energy Ocean View solar (26 x ~3-acre facilities among homes)
groups: Ann & Peter Bosted; Ocean View / Hawaiian Ranchos residents (~600-850 signature petition)
mechanism: ~600-850 signature petition; PUC complaint; testimony campaign for HB 2665 to require SUP for solar >15 kW in nonconforming residential subdivisions
outcome: HB 2665 (2018) died in House Finance without a hearing (last action 2018-02-16); PUC complaint outcome UNVERIFIED
notes: ORGANIC residents wanting MORE regulation of solar in their own residential subdivision (D/E land) -- structurally different from utility-scale-on-farmland. Counterexample to astroturf hypothesis. All testifiers identifiable local residents; zero utility/landholder/LURF presence.

project: West Kauai Energy Project (solar + pumped-storage hydro)
groups: Poai Wai Ola; Na Kiai Kai
forum: State land board (BLNR) EA process; Circuit Court (environmental-review lawsuit)
mechanism: Lawsuit over lack of EIS and rubber-stamped EA/FONSI; objection to Waimea River water diversion
outcome: KIUC dropped the stream-diversion component Dec 2023; March 2024 settlement -- KIUC withdrew the EA/FONSI and agreed to a public hearing on any future review. Project scaled down to solar-powered pumped storage only
status: Project scaled down; PV component alive
notes: Opposition targeted the HYDRO / water-diversion component (plantation-era water-rights fight), NOT the PV. Won removal of the stream-diversion element. Kauai's solar SUPs (SP15-407, SP17-408, SP26-416) drew ZERO intervenors.

project: Kauai utility-scale solar SUPs (Kapaia SP15-407; Lawai SP17-408; Kaawanui SP26-416)
forum: LUC special-use permit / Kauai Planning Commission
notes: NULL for Kauai solar. Kauai resistance appears only on the water-diversion/hydro component of WKEP, never the PV.

project: Big Wind - Lanai (up to ~200 MW, ~170 turbines) + interisland undersea cable
developer: Castle & Cooke / David Murdock; later Pattern Energy
groups: Friends of Lanai; Lanaians for Sensible Growth
mechanism: Petition to intervene; sustained community campaign against exporting Lanai wind to Oahu via cable
outcome: Project effectively dead; PUC formally closed the docket 2017-08-15 after the Legislature repealed the 2012 undersea-cable law and HECO said it no longer needed Lanai wind
notes: Organized, sustained opposition on a small island; project also collapsed on policy/economics (cable repeal). Opposition contributed to but was not the sole cause of death. Neighbor-island 'shoulder the burden for Oahu' equity frame.

project: Big Wind - Molokai (wind farm + interisland cable)
groups: I Aloha Molokai; broad Molokai community
mechanism: Sustained community opposition; call to halt Big Wind
outcome: Died: major Molokai landowner (Molokai Ranch) declined to negotiate (2011); project abandoned; docket closed with Lanai 2017
notes: Molokai's strong home-rule / self-determination stance. Opposition + landowner refusal to host both mattered. Molokai broadly resists utility-scale renewables (also relevant: Molokai New Energy Partners PPA 2018-0053, no intervention found).

project: SB 631 (2011) -- solar on B/C ag land liberalization
groups: Sierra Club Hawaii Chapter (Robert Harris; Anthony Aalto)
mechanism: Opposition testimony against liberalizing solar on prime ag land; food-security / anti-sprawl frame
outcome: Bill passed WITH the 10%/20-acre cap (Act 217, SLH 2011) over Sierra Club opposition; Club called it a 'bad bill that passed'
source_urls: https://notes/sierra-club-food-security.md (project note); testimony via capitol.hawaii.gov session2011 (blocked to scripts; UNVERIFIED re-pull)
notes: Resistance in the LEGISLATIVE forum to a permissive siting bill. Sierra Club opposition was venue-specific (ag-district integrity), same personnel/frame as its Koa Ridge/Hoopili anti-sprawl litigation. Not astroturf -- adversarial to landholders.

project: HB 593 (2019) -- solar on class-A prime ag land
groups: Dept of Agriculture; Office of Planning; Hawaii Farm Bureau; Sierra Club; Life of the Land
mechanism: Coalition opposition testimony against putting solar on A-rated prime ag land
outcome: Stiff opposition; bill did not become law (ag-policy fight, not a neighborhood one)
notes: Legislative-forum resistance to a permissive bill. Agency + ag-lobby + enviro coalition, not community-neighborhood opposition. Parallels the Hoohana boundary-amendment ag-land fight (2019, same coalition).
